22: 23: Welcome to the [Forem](https://forem.com) codebase, the platform that powers 24: [dev.to](https://dev.to). We are so excited to have you. With your help, we can 25: build out Forem’s usability, scalability, and stability to better serve our 26: communities. 27: 28: ## What is Forem? 29: 30: Forem is open source software for building communities. Communities for your 31: peers, customers, fanbases, families, friends, and any other time and space 32: where people need to come together to be part of a collective. 33: [See our announcement post](https://dev.to/devteam/for-empowering-community-2k6h) 34: for a high-level overview of what Forem is. 35: 36: [dev.to](https://dev.to) (or just DEV) is hosted by Forem. It is a community of 37: software developers who write articles, take part in discussions, and build 38: their professional profiles. We value supportive and constructive dialogue in 39: the pursuit of great code and career growth for all members. The ecosystem spans 40: from beginner to advanced developers, and all are welcome to find their place 41: within our community. ❤️ 42: 43: ## Table of Contents 44: 45: - [What is Forem?](#what-is-forem) 46: - [Table of Contents](#table-of-contents) 47: - [Contributing](#contributing) 48: - [Getting Started](#getting-started) 49: - [Installation Documentation](#installation-documentation) 50: - [Developer Documentation](#developer-documentation) 51: - [Deployment with Kamal](#deployment-with-kamal) 52: - [Vulnerability Disclosure](#vulnerability-disclosure) 53: - [Acknowledgements](#acknowledgements) 54: - [License](#license) 55: 56: 57: ## Contributing 58: 59: We welcome pull requests on any issue, regardless of its current status, if it provides a fix or improvement. While we cannot guarantee that every PR will be merged, we value legitimate suggestions and contributions. 60: 61: **All pull requests should include appropriate tests.** Our current focus is primarily on backend-related regression tests, while frontend changes may require more extensive user review. 62: 63: We welcome AI-assisted pull requests but will turn away "slop"—all submissions must maintain high standards of code quality and meaningful utility. 64: 65: 🤖 AI Agents should check out `AGENTS.md` for more instructions. For specific IDEs and environments, Forem also synchronizes these rules across `.cursorrules`, `.windsurfrules`, and `.github/copilot-instructions.md`. 66: 67: Please also check out the [Contributing to Forem guide](https://developers.forem.com/contributing-guide/forem) for more detailed guidelines. 68: 69: ## Getting Started 70: 71: This section provides a high-level quick start guide. If you're looking for a 72: more thorough installation guide (for example 73: [with macOS](https://developers.forem.com/getting-started/installation/mac)), 74: you'll want to refer to our complete 75: [Developer Documentation](https://developers.forem.com/). 76: 77: We run on a [Rails](https://rubyonrails.org/) backend, and we are currently 78: transitioning to a [Preact](https://preactjs.com/)-first frontend. 79: 80: **Prerequisites Note**: Forem now utilizes advanced AI embeddings for feed generation. You must ensure your PostgreSQL installation has the `pgvector` extension (version 0.8.0 or higher) installed to support HNSW indexing. This database extension is a **hard requirement** to migrate the database and run the app. 81: 82: To install `pgvector`: 83: - **macOS (Homebrew)**: `brew install pgvector` 84: - **Linux (Debian/Ubuntu)**: `sudo apt install postgresql-15-pgvector` (adjust `15` to match your installed PostgreSQL version), or compile from source following the [pgvector installation guide](https://github.com/pgvector/pgvector#installation). 85: 86: However, providing a Gemini API key (`GEMINI_API_KEY`) to actually generate the embeddings is completely optional; if omitted, the app will continue to function normally without semantic recommendations. 87: 88: A more complete overview of our stack is available in 89: [our docs](https://developers.forem.com/technical-overview/stack). 90: 91: To **launch Forem in Gitpod**, please navigate to 92: [https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/{your_github_username}/forem](https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/{your_github_username}/forem). 93: 94: To **launch Forem in Ona** (formerly Gitpod), the project is fully configured for Ona development environments. Simply open the project in Ona and the environment will be automatically configured with all necessary services and dependencies. 95: 96: ### Installation Documentation 97: 98: Please see our installation guides: 99: 100: - [macOS, without containers](https://developers.forem.com/getting-started/installation/mac) 101: - [Linux, without containers](https://developers.forem.com/getting-started/installation/linux) 102: 103: ## Developer Documentation 104: 105: [Check out our dedicated docs page for more technical documentation](https://developers.forem.com). Please note that while the documentation is a great place to start, some parts may be out of date as the project evolves. 106: 107: ## Deployment with Kamal (Beta) 108: 109: Forem is equipped with [Kamal 2](https://kamal-deploy.org/) to allow you to easily deploy the application to any cloud provider or bare metal server. 110: 111: For a comprehensive guide, including cloud provider setups (AWS, DigitalOcean, Hetzner, GCP), secrets management, and Forem-specific production gotchas, see the [Kamal Deployment Guide](docs/kamal-deployment.md). 112: 113: ### Prerequisites 114: 115: Before deploying, ensure you have: 116: 1. SSH access to your target server(s). 117: 2. Docker installed on your local machine and target server(s). 118: 3. A Docker container registry account (e.g., GitHub Container Registry, Docker Hub). 119: 120: ### Configuration 121: 122: 1. **Deployment Configuration**: Open [config/deploy.yml](config/deploy.yml) and update: 123: - Your registry username/credentials. 124: - Your domain name in the `proxy` section. 125: 2. **Environment & Secrets Configuration**: Set the following environment variables on your local machine: 126: - `KAMAL_WEB_IP` / `KAMAL_JOB_IP` / `KAMAL_DB_IP` / `KAMAL_REDIS_IP`: The IP addresses of your servers (default to `192.168.0.1`). 127: - `KAMAL_REGISTRY_PASSWORD`: Access token for your Docker container registry. 128: - `RAILS_MASTER_KEY`: Your Rails master key. 129: - `DATABASE_URL`: Connection string for PostgreSQL. This is always required. If you are using the Kamal postgres accessory, set it to the accessory hostname (e.g. `postgresql://postgres:password@forem-postgres:5432/forem_production`). 130: - `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`: Root password for the PostgreSQL accessory (if using it). 131: 132: ### Deploying the Application 133: 134: Run the following commands to set up and deploy the application: 135: 136: ```bash 137: # 1. Verify your configuration is valid 138: bundle exec kamal config 139: 140: # 2. Boot accessories, build & push the docker image, and deploy the application 141: bundle exec kamal setup 142: 143: # 3. 1: module Reactable
2:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
3: 
4:   included do
5:     has_many :reactions, as: :reactable, inverse_of: :reactable, dependent: :destroy
6:     has_many :distinct_reaction_categories, -> { order(:category).merge(Reaction.distinct_categories) },
7:              as: :reactable,
8:              inverse_of: :reactable,
9:              dependent: nil,
10:              class_name: "Reaction"
11:   end
12: 
13:   class_methods do
14:     # Batch sync reaction counts for multiple records efficiently
15:     # Uses transaction + row locking to prevent race conditions
16:     # Eliminates N+1 queries by using a single grouped query + batch update
17:     #
18:     # @param records [Array<ActiveRecord::Base>] records to sync
19:     # @return [Integer] number of records updated
20:     def sync_reactions_count_for_batch(records)
21:       return 0 if records.empty?
22: 
23:       ids = records.map(&:id)
24: 
25:       transaction do
26:         # Lock the rows to prevent concurrent updates (SKIP LOCKED for non-blocking)
27:         where(id: ids).lock("FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED").load
28: 
29:         # Single grouped query to get correct counts for all records
30:         correct_counts = Reaction
31:           .where(reactable_type: name, reactable_id: ids)
32:           .public_category
33:           .group(:reactable_id)
34:           .count
35: 
36:         # Use Arel for safe SQL generation instead of string interpolation
37:         update_cases = ids.map do |record_id|
38:           count = correct_counts[record_id] || 0
39:           Arel.sql("WHEN #{connection.quote(record_id)} THEN #{count.to_i}")
40:         end
41: 
42:         # Perform batch update using ActiveRecord with sanitized Arel
43:         where(id: ids).update_all(
44:           Arel.sql(
45:             "public_reactions_count = CASE id #{update_cases.join(' ')} END",
46:           ),
47:         )
48:       end
49: 
50:       ids.size
51:     end
52:   end
53: 
54:   def sync_reactions_count
55:     # Use transaction + row locking to prevent race conditions
56:     self.class.transaction do
57:       self.class.where(id: id).lock("FOR UPDATE").load
58:       correct_count = reactions.public_category.count
59:       self.class.where(id: id).update_all(public_reactions_count: correct_count)
60:     end
61:   end
62: 
63:   # Thread-safe sync that can be called from background jobs
64:   def sync_reactions_count!
65:     sync_reactions_count
66:     reload
67:   end
68: 
69:   def public_reaction_categories
70:     # Get reaction counts for this reactable
71:     reaction_counts = reaction_counts_for_reactable
72: 
73:     # Filter to only public reaction categories that have reactions
74:     categories_with_counts = reaction_counts.select do |count_data|
75:       category_slug = count_data[:category]
76:       count_data[:count].positive? && ReactionCategory[category_slug]&.visible_to_public?
77:     end
78: 
79:     # Sort by count (descending), then by position for ties, and limit to 3
80:     sorted_categories = categories_with_counts
81:       .sort_by { |count_data| [-count_data[:count], ReactionCategory[count_data[:category]]&.position || 99] }
82:       .first(3)
83: 
84:     # Convert back to ReactionCategory objects
85:     sorted_categories.map do |count_data|
86:       ReactionCategory[count_data[:category]]
87:     end
88:   end
89: 
90:   private
91: 
92:   def reaction_counts_for_reactable
93:     return [] unless respond_to?(:id)
94: 
95:     Rails.cache.fetch("reaction_counts_for_reactable-#{self.class.name}-#{id}", expires_in: 10.hours) do
96:       reactions.group(:category).count.map do |category, count|
97:         { category: category, count: count }
98:       end
99:     end
100:   end
101: end

15:   #              presence could create some havoc with our edge caching.  So I'm scoping it to the
16:   #              place where the code is likely to raise an ApplicationPolicy::UserRequiredError.
17:   #
18:   #              I still want to enable this, but first want to get things mostly conformant with
19:   #              existing expectations.  Note, in config/application.rb, we're rescuing the below
20:   #              exception as though it was a Pundit::NotAuthorizedError.
21:   #
22:   #              The difference being that rescue_from is an ALWAYS use case.  Whereas the
23:   #              config/application.rb uses the config.consider_all_requests_local to determine if
24:   #              we bubble the exception up or handle it.
25:   #

28:   def feed
29:     # [@jeremyf] - I am a firm believer that we should check authorization.  However, in this case,
30:     #              based on our implementation constraints and assumptions, the `#feed` action will
31:     #              almost certainly be available to everyone (what's in the feed will vary
32:     #              signficantly).  So while I would love an `authorize(Article)` here, I will make
33:     #              do with a comment.
34:     skip_authorization
35: 
36:     @articles = Article.feed.order(published_at: :desc).page(params[:page].to_i).per(12)
37:     @latest = request.path == latest_feed_path
38:     @articles = if params[:username]
39:                   handle_user_or_organization_feed
40:                 elsif params[:tag]
41:                   handle_tag_feed
42:                 elsif @latest
43:                   @articles
44:                     .where("score > ?", Articles::Feeds::Latest::MINIMUM_SCORE)
45:                     .includes(:user)
46:                 else

48:                     .with_at_least_home_feed_minimum_score
49:                     .includes(:user)
50:                 end
51:

58:                 end

60:     set_cache_control_headers(10.minutes.to_i, stale_while_revalidate: 30, stale_if_error: 1.day.to_i)
61: 
62:     render layout: false, content_type: "application/xml", locals: {
63:       articles: @articles,
64:       user: @user,
65:       tag: @tag,
66:       latest: @latest,
